summary.notes: Broad serendipity scan of HN comment recommendations; deduped against ~/.hermes/hn-scout/product-history.json.

items.name: Claude Design
items.summary: Anthropic's design-generation feature that turns prompts into mockups and layouts.
items.why_included: Commenters treated it as a genuinely useful way to communicate intent quickly, not just a novelty demo.
items.evidence: Agency users said it can replace long reference-hunting and 45-minute calls by producing a usable mockup in about 30 seconds, though several noted it still will not replace Figma.

items.name: Smol machines
items.source_story_title: Show HN: Smol machines – subsecond coldstart, portable virtual machines
items.summary: Portable VMs with container-like ergonomics and subsecond cold starts.
items.why_included: The thread read like an enthusiastic early-adopter discussion of a genuinely new runtime and packaging approach.
items.evidence: The founder described it as a Docker replacement with Firecracker-inspired isolation, and commenters praised the comparison table, self-contained binary packaging, and overall design.

items.name: Healthchecks.io
items.source_story_title: Healthchecks.io now uses self-hosted object storage
items.summary: A long-running monitoring service moved to self-hosted object storage to keep infrastructure simple.
items.why_included: The discussion highlighted a pragmatic, low-drama backend choice that other self-hosters can copy.
items.evidence: Users called Healthchecks.io fantastic, praised the one-person two-drive rsync setup, and pointed to Garage and Versity S3 as viable self-hosted storage options.

items.name: Garage
items.source_story_title: Healthchecks.io now uses self-hosted object storage
items.summary: Open-source, self-hosted S3-compatible object storage recommended in the Healthchecks discussion.
items.why_included: It was a concrete infrastructure recommendation people could adopt immediately.
items.evidence: A commenter explicitly told S3 self-hosters to check out Garage and linked the project as a viable alternative.

items.name: PanicLock
items.source_story_title: Show HN: PanicLock – Close your MacBook lid disable TouchID –> password unlock
items.summary: A small macOS utility that disables Touch ID after lid close so password unlock is required.
items.why_included: Commenters saw it as a real security and privacy workflow, not a gimmick.
items.evidence: People called it a great idea and a 2026 version of a boss key; one comment said it solves the discomfort of typing passwords in public.

items.name: Stage
items.source_story_title: Show HN: Stage – Putting humans back in control of code review
items.summary: An AI-assisted code review app that keeps humans in the loop while summarizing changes.
items.why_included: The thread connected directly to a real pain point in AI-assisted dev workflows.
items.evidence: Commenters compared it with devon.ai and ReviewStack, debated whether local tools beat SaaS, and said they still wanted the code itself more than another AI summary.

items.name: NeoGeo AES+
items.source_story_title: NeoGeo AES+: SNK announces reissue of retro console without emulation
items.summary: SNK is reissuing the Neo Geo AES without emulation, reviving a classic cartridge-based console.
items.why_included: It drew the kind of hardware nostalgia and technical scrutiny that makes retro launches worth surfacing.
items.evidence: Commenters called it extremely cool, discussed cartridge pricing versus the used market, and compared the approach with FPGA alternatives like MISTerFPGA.
